sections.paragraph: ἄβυσσος, ου, ἡ [ἀ-priv., βυσσός = βυθός ‘depth’ esp. of the sea; ‘something of such depth that it appears beyond standard measurement’] ‘a transcendent region deep below the earth’s surface’, abyss, underworld, of the deep abode of the dead Ro 10:7; of demons Lk 8:31; of an apocalyptic beast Rv 11:7.
